— TJ^fWT «<'*|0 <I^H5) ^" SlfiWJ^Hr +iWI 28-10/45-T^ ( 1 ) mfrs 21 ft^T, 1945 %^RT M*lR(lfl f3^T «7T3fk ^ ^ ' f*TS5# ^ K U frr.^T.f=T. 311(3T), TfRt^ST 1-5-2002 %?TO W t * R fsfWFtT I 1-5-1979 T W W R T O W W •afNftr aik •jraitPT ••HIHUI 'PPPT, 1945 " ^ R ^ T afR Tri^nr oh^i") H S I H I (€qnf«^ 'Prmn) % W*(?H, I^Rrtf 3fhrf*r ( i ) -- 1 of 4 -- 2 - ^ j p r c GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3 (i)] MINISTRY OFHEALTH AND FAMILY WELFARE (Department of Indian System of Medicine arid Homoeopathy) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 11th June, 2002 G.S.R. 422(E).—Whereas a draft ofcertam rales further to amend the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945, which the Central Government, after consultation with the Ayurvedic, Siddha and Unani Drugs Technical Ajdvisory Board, proposes to make, in exercise of the powers conferred by clause (gga) of sub-section (2) of section 3 3 -N of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (23 of 194b), was published, as required by the said section, at page 4 to 5 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i), dated the 6th June 2001, under the notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (Department of Indian Systems of Medicine & Homoeopathy) number 419(E), dated the 6th June, 2001, inviting objections and suggestions from all persons likely to be affected thereby before expiry of a period forty- five days from the date on which the copies of the Official Gazette containing the said notification were made available to the public; And whereas copies of the said Gazette were made available to the public on the 6th June, 2001; And whereas no objections and suggestions from the public on the said draft rules have been received by the Central Government; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 33-N of the said Act, the CeHtral Government, after consultation with the Ayurveda, Siddha and Unani Drags Technical Advisory Board, hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945, namely :— 1. (1) TheserulesmaybccalledtheDrugsandCosmeticsRules(4thAmendment),2002. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945,inrulc 168, in the column relating to "Class of drugs", against item 1, for the word "Single drugs", the word "Drugs" shall be substituted. [F. No. K11023/44/20(XH>CC(ISM)] L. PRASAD, Jt Secy. Foot note:—The principal rules were published in the Official Gazette vide notification No. F.28-10/45-H(l) dated the 21st December, 1945 and the last amended vide G. S.R. No. 311(E) dated the 1 -5-2002. Tran;, JH'^M ^rf*ro t||< RlU)U|:—TJST f^PW «<*l6 V>IH5| ^' 3TftRi«HI Wm 28-10/45-13^ (1) TIK^ST 21 fttW, 1945 %5RI HWRllfl ^3TT «5B;3?k ^ f f t w f r w i . m f t WIT311 (3T), Tnfhsr 1-5-2002 %sro^tt«R foi*u Jwi 11-5-1979 ^^eiravilfafl affafa afa; "SraTtpT yiHlft ft^q, 1945 ^ T ^ T sfiT Hl^R «r>Wlul "RIflWfy (TTT^ir'IVfPT) % HtilifH, pTO^'aiWftr -sftr •srarKpr •HiHifl srfaf^tw, 1940 ("tft^t'afl"^i^T-6i) t , •$ sitfftud 11 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 11th June, 2002 G.S.R. 423(E).—Whereas a draft of certain amendments further to amend the First Schedule to the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (23 of 1940), which the Central Government, after consultation with the Ayurvedic, Siddha and Unani Drugs Technical Advisory Board, proposes to make, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 33-0 of the said Act, was published, as required by the said section, at pages 2 to 3 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub- section (i), dated the 6th June, 2001, under the notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (Department of Indian Systems of Medicine & Homoeopathy) number G.S.R. 418(E), dated the 6th June, 2001, inviting objections and suggestions from all persons likely to be affected thereby, before the expiry of a period of three months from the date on which the copies of the Official Gazette containing the said notification were made available to the public; And whereas copies of the said Gazette were made available to the public on the 6th June, 2001; And whereas no objections and suggestions from the public on the said draft amendments have been received by the Central Government; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 3 3 -O of the said Act, the Central Government, after consultation with the Ayurveda, Siddha and Unani Drugs Technical Advisory Board, hereby makes the following further amendments to the First Schedule to the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940, namely :— In the First Schedule to the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (23 of 1940), under the heading "Ayurveda",— (a) in the entry against serial number 54 A, the brackets, word and figure "(Part-I)" shall be omitted; (b) after serial number 54 B and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be inserted, namely :— "54 C-Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India." [F. No. K. llC23/44/7000-DCCaSM)] L. PRASAD, Jt. Secy. Foot note:— The principal rules were published in the Official Gazette vide notification No. F, 28-10/45-H( 1) dated the 21st December, 1945 and the last amended vide G.S.R. No. 311 (E) dated the 1-5-2002, The Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945 as amended up to 1-5-1979 is contained in the publication of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (Department of Health) containing the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (PDGHS-61).
